title: Viscera Cleanup Detail

notes:
The guides posted here did not work without some extra steps, so I'm posting an updated guide. Hopefully this will work for you.

FORCE USE OF PROTON 3.7-8 FOR THIS GAME OR THIS MAY NOT WORK!

1. Remove the dotNetFx40_Full_setup.exe file in Binaries/InstallData directory.

2. Run the game once in 32-bit so the prefix is built.

3. Run "protontricks 246900 dotnet452" in a terminal, and choose to remove the .NET framework.

4. After that's done, run "protontricks 246900 --force dotnet452" in a terminal. Without the --force argument, it'll complain that dotnet40 is already installed.

5. Download the 64-bit Visual C++ Redistributable for Visual Studio 2012 Update 4 from here: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=30679

6. Run "protontricks 246900" in a terminal, and select "Select the default wineprefix", followed by "Run taskmgr".

7. In the Task Manager, start a new task and navigate to where you downloaded the redistributable, then run it.

8. Select "Uninstall" from the options, and wait for it to finish.

9. Once that's done, run it again to install the redistributable.

10. You should be done! Run the game in 64-bit. It will sometimes throw up an error box saying that rundll32.exe could not be started. Just click "No" to bypass, and click it again if it decides to throw it up again.  WARNING: In-game volume controls do not work, the game may be very loud! Use your volume mixer to adjust the volume.

Performance is somewhat choppy, but it is fully playable with the exception of some Workshop maps, which crash to desktop for some reason.

notes:
Install the game, then go in the local files and delete the redist file as well as any file with dotnet in the name. Then install protontricks and use it to install dotnet452 ("protontricks 246900 dotnet452"). Launch the game in 64 mode, and here you go :)

Also, some weird graphic glitch may appear the first time you play, try messing with the resolution or putting the game in fullscreen. Other than that, the game work well.

notes: Viscera Cleanup Detail now works perfectly without tweaks.  However, if you have previously attempted to run this title with earlier versions of Proton, you should uninstall the game *and* delete the compat data (rm -rf ~/.steam/steamapps/compatdata/246900) before doing a fresh install, otherwise the game will not start.

notes: Installs fine (despite the error message). Both 32 and 64 bit versions run without issue. The game is perfectly playable, though suffers from poor FPS in some areas (even on minimum settings). The game is definitely playable, and the framerate is consistent, even when slow. Looking forward to seeing this run on top of Vulkan.

notes: Works great out of the box, 32 and 64 bit. Running experimental D9VK by setting PROTON_USE_D9VK=1, I can max out ALL video settings and still get that sweet 60 FPS. This resulted in a couple minor lighting glitches, but I'm not sure if those were there without the vulkan layer, nor does it have any impact on the gameplay.

title: Viscera Cleanup Detail

notes: It works out of the box, despite the missing file errors during the first installation process. Adding "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" (without the quotes) to the launch options will enable D9VK and make the game run as fast as it runs in Windows. However, with D9VK enabled, the DLCs -the "games" that you can acess from "Switch Game" option from the main menu- will crash. When you want to play the DLCs just change the launch option with this: "PROTON_USE_D9VK=0 %command%" (without the quotes again) to disable the D9VK for the time being. After you are done with DLCs, I recommend you to re-enable D9VK as the performance loss that you get without D9VK is annoying at times. I didn't try Workshop content.
title: Viscera Cleanup Detail
